actions.label: Form LLC & Set Up Books
actions.description: Do the full foundation in one move: form your LLC, open a business bank account, and set up bookkeeping. Your business becomes a real legal entity with clean, separate finances.
actions.lesson: Separate yourself from the business from day one. The LLC shields your personal assets, the business account stops commingling, and bookkeeping lets you prove income to lenders — the foundation everything else is built on.
actions.narrative_success: The LLC is filed, the EIN issued, a business checking account is open, and your books are live. Your business exists as its own legal person with clean financials — and bookkeeping runs about $150/mo from here.
actions.narrative_failure: The LLC is filed and the account is open, but the bookkeeping setup is half-done — categories are a work in progress. Still running about $150/mo.

actions.label: Build Personal Credit
actions.description: Hire a credit pro to dispute negative marks and clear collections, then add tradelines and report rent and utilities to thicken your file. Repair and optimize in one push.
actions.lesson: Your credit score is an asset that prints access to cheap money. Clear the bad marks and build depth — a few points decide whether the bank says yes, and at what rate. Build it before you need it.
actions.narrative_success: Two collections removed, a late payment deleted, two tradelines added, and rent reporting switched on. Your file looks thicker and cleaner — your score jumped 25 points.
actions.narrative_failure: One dispute landed and a tradeline is pending. Small movement now, but the foundation is set for next month.

actions.label: Aggressive Debt Paydown
actions.description: Throw every spare dollar at your highest-interest debt. It hurts now, frees you later.
actions.lesson: Know the difference: high-interest consumer debt is a wealth-killer — clear it fast. Productive, asset-backed debt is a tool. Eliminate the bad so you can safely use the good.
actions.narrative_success: Two thousand dollars gone from the balance. The interest savings alone will compound from here.
actions.narrative_failure: Paid down most of it. An unexpected expense ate part of the payment. Still progress.

actions.label: Restructure & Optimize Your Debt
actions.description: The full debt playbook with a lending expert: open a 0% business line, use an installment loan to pay down your cards (utilization drops), shift the balance onto the business line so it's off your personal report, and pull a little working cash. Replaces the standalone credit-line and loan actions.
actions.lesson: The optimal debt move in one sequence: revolving → installment drops your utilization, then moving it onto business credit takes it off your personal report entirely, and the 0% intro buys time. Same debt, restructured into a higher score and usable cash.
actions.narrative_failure: The expert moved one balance and opened a starter business line, but couldn't restructure everything yet. Your file is building — reapply in a month for better terms.

actions.label: Get a Bank Loan
actions.description: Walk into your bank and apply for a personal or small business loan. Good credit opens doors — even without an LLC.
actions.lesson: Debt isn't the enemy — UNPRODUCTIVE debt is. Borrowing to grow a business that pays you back is how you buy freedom in years instead of decades. The 'safe' path — a salaried job — trades your time for the most heavily-taxed income there is.
actions.narrative_success: Approved for $10,000. The loan officer barely hesitated — your credit spoke for itself. Cash in account by Friday.
actions.narrative_failure: Declined. The loan officer said your debt-to-income was borderline. Try paying down some debt first.

actions.label: Open Business Line of Credit
actions.description: Get approved for a revolving credit line in the business name. Cash when you need it, interest only when you use it.
actions.lesson: Business credit borrows on the company's name and assets — not yours. This is how you scale with other people's money without putting your household on the line.
actions.narrative_success: Approved for $15,000. The banker shook your hand and said 'come back when you need more.' You will.
actions.narrative_failure: Approved, but only for $5,000. Your revenue history is too short for more. Build the relationship.

actions.label: Apply for Additional Credit Lines
actions.description: With your improved credit profile, apply for additional business credit lines and cards. More available credit means lower utilization and more runway.
actions.lesson: Unused credit lowers your utilization, raises your score, and sits ready as dry powder for the right opportunity. Access is an asset even when idle.
actions.narrative_success: Approved for $15,000 across two new business credit lines. Your available credit just tripled. Utilization dropped to single digits.
actions.narrative_failure: One card approved for $3,000, the other declined. The hard inquiry dinged your score slightly. Your expert says wait 90 days and try again.

actions.label: Secure a Business Term Loan
actions.description: Apply for a fixed-rate business loan through your banking relationship. Use it to consolidate revolving debt, fund growth, or build cash reserves.
actions.lesson: Fixed-rate, predictable debt is a planning tool. Use it to retire expensive revolving balances or fund growth you can confidently pay back — that's productive debt.
actions.narrative_success: Loan approved — $25,000 at 8.5% fixed. The banker smiled when she handed you the docs. Your credit line utilization just dropped to nearly zero, and you have cash to deploy.
actions.narrative_failure: Declined. Revenue history is too short, or DSCR was borderline. Your lending expert says shore up three more months of financials and reapply.

actions.label: Build Banking Relationship
actions.description: Meet with your banker regularly. Move deposits through their bank. Become a known quantity before you need a loan.
actions.lesson: Capital is a relationship business. Bankers fund people they know and trust — build the relationship before you need money, not when you're desperate for it.
actions.narrative_success: The branch manager knows your name now. When you need capital, you won't be a stranger walking in cold.
actions.narrative_failure: Good meeting, but the banker transferred branches. You're starting the relationship over with someone new.

actions.label: Business Insurance Stack
actions.description: The business buys three policies: income protection (if you can't work, the bills still get paid), key-person life insurance (a payout to the company if it loses you), and general business liability (lawsuits hit the policy, not your balance sheet). All paid by the business.
actions.lesson: Protect the engine before you build on it. Income protection covers your earning power, key-person life insurance pays the company if it loses you — the one life policy the business itself buys — and liability coverage absorbs the lawsuits that come with growth. These are business expenses, deducted before tax.
actions.narrative_success: Three policies in force, all on the business books. Income protection covers your family for two years if you can't work, the key-person payout would fund your replacement, and the liability policy stands between a lawsuit and everything you've built. Premiums run about $550/mo.
actions.narrative_failure: Applications submitted; underwriting wants more medical records on one. The other two are active. Should fully clear next month.

actions.label: Elect S-Corp & Operating Agreement
actions.description: Upgrade your LLC: put yourself on real payroll, adopt a proper operating agreement, and elect S-Corp taxation to cut self-employment tax.
actions.lesson: How your income is STRUCTURED matters as much as how much you earn. A documented salary plus S-Corp election splits salary from distributions and legally cuts self-employment tax — and the operating agreement hardens your liability shield. Levers a W-2 employee never gets.
actions.narrative_success: You're on payroll, the operating agreement is signed, and the S-Corp election is filed. Your tax advisor estimates $8,000–$12,000 in annual savings — and lenders now see a real, bankable business.
actions.narrative_failure: Payroll is running and the election is filed, but at a lower salary than planned. Savings are real, just smaller until revenue catches up.

actions.label: Asset Protection Stack
actions.description: Layer a holding company over your operating business, then add an irrevocable trust and umbrella policy. Lawsuits hit the operating company; your accumulated wealth sits untouchable above it.
actions.lesson: A holding company owns the assets; the operating company takes the risk. Add a trust and umbrella and one lawsuit can't erase a decade of building. The wealthy protect first, then grow.
actions.narrative_success: A holding company now owns the operating business, and the attorney delivered the trust documents. Your assets are held by entities that don't have your name on the door. Bulletproof.
actions.narrative_failure: The holding structure is filed and a basic shield is in place, but the full trust setup needs more time. Partially protected.

actions.label: Advanced Tax Strategy
actions.description: Engage a tax strategist year-round: max retirement vehicles, cost segregation, deductions and entity optimization. Legal, aggressive, effective.
actions.lesson: Taxes are most people's single largest lifetime expense. Year-round planning — not an April scramble — legally minimizes them. That's the difference between building wealth and funding someone else's budget.
actions.narrative_success: Your effective tax rate dropped from 32% to 21%. Retirement vehicles maxed, cost segregation done, deductions captured — all legal. Your CPA high-fived you.
actions.narrative_failure: Some strategies implemented, others need documentation. Partial savings this year, full effect next.

actions.label: Set Up Monthly Tax Reserve
actions.description: Automatically set aside a percentage of revenue each month for taxes. No more year-end surprises.
actions.lesson: Set aside taxes every month and tax season stops being a crisis. That money was never yours — treat it that way and you'll never get blindsided.
actions.narrative_success: Tax reserve is active. Every month, a slice of revenue goes into a separate pot. Future you will be grateful.
actions.narrative_failure: Set up started but your bookkeeping needs cleanup first. Should be running next month.

actions.label: Open Accumulation Insurance Policy
actions.description: Set up an indexed universal life (IUL) policy that builds tax-advantaged cash value earning ~7% annually. Use it as an emergency fund, tax reserve, or retirement vehicle.
actions.lesson: This is your tax-advantaged money engine. Cash value grows tax-free, you can borrow against it anytime, and at scale it throws off passive, tax-free income. The earlier you start it, the more time compounding has to work for you.
actions.narrative_success: Policy in force. Your first $3,000 is in the cash value. It'll grow at 7% tax-free, and you can borrow against up to 90% of it anytime — no bank approval needed.
actions.narrative_failure: Underwriting needs more info. Should be approved next month.

actions.label: Start Monthly Policy Funding
actions.description: Set up an automatic monthly contribution into your accumulation policy — sized to your revenue, cash, and available business credit. About 2% of each contribution covers the cost of insurance; the rest compounds tax-free at ~7%/yr and grows your borrowing base.
actions.lesson: Keep feeding the engine on autopilot. A fixed monthly contribution (minus a small ~2% cost of insurance) compounds tax-free and steadily grows the cash value you'll later borrow against for passive, tax-free income. Consistency beats lump sums.

actions.label: Activate Tax-Free Passive Income
actions.description: Turn on a monthly income stream from your insurance policy. The insurer pays you through policy loans against your cash value — no credit check, no taxes. The cash value keeps compounding at ~7%/yr; the loan accrues ~5%/yr but you never pay it — it's simply netted from the death benefit. The balance grows under Loans with zero impact on your money today.
actions.lesson: This is the crown jewel. You draw tax-free income as a loan against your policy while the cash value keeps growing — the 7% growth outruns the 5% loan cost, so the gap funds your lifestyle forever. The loan is repaid from the death benefit, not your pocket. Income without work, without tax, without ever selling the asset.
actions.narrative_success: The income switch is on. Each month the insurer wires you a tax-free check drawn against your cash value — and your policy keeps compounding as if you never touched it. You're being paid to own the asset.

actions.label: Take a Policy Loan
actions.description: Borrow against your insurance cash value — up to 90%. No credit check, no bank approval, no impact on your credit score. The cash value keeps growing even while borrowed against.
actions.lesson: You just accessed cash tax-free — no bank, no credit check — and your money kept compounding as if you never touched it. This is how the wealthy spend without selling assets or triggering a tax bill. You became your own bank.
actions.narrative_success: Policy loan approved instantly — no credit check, no application. The money is in your account tomorrow, and your cash value keeps compounding as if you never touched it.

actions.label: Premium Financing Strategy
actions.description: Use borrowed money to fund a large whole life policy. Cash value grows tax-free. The bank's money, your wealth.
actions.lesson: The wealthy borrow at low rates to fund tax-free assets. Using the bank's money to build cash value that compounds tax-free is leverage applied to the money engine itself.
actions.narrative_success: The premium financing deal is structured. You're building a tax-free asset with the bank's money. This is how the wealthy stay wealthy.
actions.narrative_failure: Partially funded. The bank's terms were tighter than expected. Still building cash value, just slower.

actions.label: Buy Income-Producing Property
actions.description: Purchase a rental property or small commercial building. Monthly cashflow plus appreciation plus tax benefits.
actions.lesson: This is leverage. You control a six-figure income-producing asset with a fraction of your own cash — the bank funds the rest, the tenant pays it down, and it appreciates while you sleep. Paying all cash would be the INEFFICIENT move.
actions.narrative_success: Keys in hand. The duplex cash-flows $700/month after mortgage, taxes, and insurance. Passive income is real now.
actions.narrative_failure: The property closed, but the inspection found issues. Repairs ate into your projections. It'll cash-flow, just not as much.

actions.label: Become a Private Lender
actions.description: Lend your capital to other entrepreneurs' deals. Earn interest like a bank — 8-15% secured by their assets.
actions.lesson: Now YOU'RE the bank. Your capital earns double-digit returns secured by someone else's assets — money working hard while you don't lift a finger. That's passive income.
actions.narrative_success: First deal funded — $20,000 at 12%, secured by the borrower's equipment. Monthly checks arriving. You're the bank now.
actions.narrative_failure: The deal funded, but the borrower's been slow on payments. Secured, so you're protected — but it's stressful.

actions.label: Acquire a Competitor
actions.description: Buy a smaller competitor as an investment. Its cash flow becomes your passive income, and the deal's depreciation and goodwill amortization become write-offs that cut your taxes.
actions.lesson: At scale you buy income, not just customers. A well-structured acquisition pays you twice: passive cash flow from day one, plus depreciation and amortization write-offs that legally shrink your tax bill. The deal funds itself.
